analyst_briefing: Anthropic's Claude Opus 4.6 advances confirmed via METR 12-hour horizon doubling, corroborated by two Tier 3 sources (Understanding AI, Redwood), holding advancing posture without tier upgrade per conservative threshold lacking lab-independent verification. Other labs (OpenAI, Google DeepMind, Meta, xAI, Chinese labs) hold stable with no new evidence. EU AI Act on track but Standards Vacuum persists ahead of August 2026 high-risk obligations (Tier 1 confirmed), flagging ESA medium urgency for sovereignty risks; US EO, UK, China unchanged. Risk match on benchmark-reality gap (20-40% accuracy drop) highlights deployment alignment issues (Medium confidence). Single lab posture change: Anthropic increased capability tempo. No escalations to FCW/SCEM/WDM; ERM low urgency on inefficiency-energy link. No contested findings. Capability velocity outpacing governance in benchmarks, but energy/inference economics constrain scaling signals absent.
